name: TraderSync
description_short: AI-enhanced trading journal with Cypher AI assistant, market replay, and comprehensive analytics across all asset classes.
description_long:
## What TraderSync Is and Who Makes It

TraderSync is an AI-enhanced trading journal built for active traders who want to move beyond spreadsheets and basic P&L tracking. Founded in 2018 and headquartered in Austin, Texas, TraderSync LLC positions itself as one of the more analytics-heavy journals in the category, sitting alongside competitors like Tradervue, TradeZella, and Edgewonk. The platform supports stocks, options, futures, forex, and crypto, which makes it one of the broader multi-asset journals on the market.

The headline feature — and the one TraderSync leans on hardest in its marketing — is **Cypher AI**, a conversational assistant that reviews your trades, flags patterns, and answers questions in plain English. It's the kind of feature that sounds like a gimmick until you actually use it on a few hundred trades and realize you've been ignoring the same three mistakes for six months.

## Core Features

At its core, TraderSync is a **trading journal** with four things bolted on that most competitors don't have all at once: broker auto-imports, market replay, deep analytics, and Cypher AI. Broker integration is broad — most US brokers and prop firm platforms sync automatically, which matters if you're logging hundreds of executions a week and don't want to touch a CSV.

The **market replay** feature is where TraderSync separates itself. On the Pro plan you get 1-minute tick replay; Premium moves to 1-second tick precision; Elite offers what TraderSync calls "ultra-precise" replay. For scalpers and day traders reviewing entries and exits at a granular level, 1-second replay is genuinely useful — most journals don't offer replay at all, and the ones that do typically stop at 1-minute candles.

Analytics cover the standard set (win rate, R-multiples, MAE/MFE, hold times, setup breakdowns, day-of-week patterns) plus tag-based filtering that lets you slice performance by strategy, symbol, or custom labels. Premium adds sector reports, which is useful for equity traders looking for concentration risk.

**Cypher AI** is the interesting piece. It's rate-limited — 5 messages per day on Pro, 15 on Premium, unlimited on Elite — which tells you TraderSync knows the token cost is real. Used well, it can surface things like "you lose 60% of the time when you enter after 2pm ET on FOMC days," which is the kind of insight that takes hours to dig out manually.

## Pricing Breakdown

TraderSync does **not offer a free tier**, which is worth stating upfront. The three paid plans are:

**Pro — $29.95/month or $233.52/year** (roughly $19.46/mo billed annually). Includes 1 trading account, 5 strategies, 1-minute market replay, 5 daily Cypher AI messages, and basic analytics. This is the entry point for a single-account trader who mostly wants a journal with a bit of AI.

**Premium — $49.95/month or $359.64/year** (roughly $29.97/mo billed annually). Up to 20 accounts, unlimited strategies, 1-second market replay, 15 Cypher AI messages per day, sector reports, and advanced analytics. This is the plan most prop firm traders and serious retail traders will actually want.

**Elite — $79.95/month or $479.40/year** (roughly $39.95/mo billed annually). Adds ultra-precise replay, advanced AI tools, unlimited Cypher AI messages, and priority support.

The annual pricing is a real discount — Elite billed annually is exactly half the monthly rate — so if you're committed for a year, always take the annual plan. Verify current pricing on TraderSync's website before subscribing, as tiers occasionally shift.

## Honest Pros

**Cypher AI is genuinely useful, not a gimmick.** Conversational trade review beats scrolling through 200 rows of your own history. It's not going to make you a better trader by itself, but it surfaces patterns you'd otherwise miss.

**1-second market replay is rare in this category.** Most trading journals give you charts or nothing. If you're a scalper or day trader who needs to relive an entry tick-by-tick, this alone can justify the Premium plan.

**Broad multi-asset support.** Stocks, options, futures, forex, and crypto in one journal. If you trade multiple asset classes, you're not paying for two subscriptions.

**Up to 20 accounts on Premium** is a big deal for prop firm traders juggling FTMO, Topstep, Apex, and personal accounts. Most competitors cap at 3–5 accounts on their mid-tier plan.

**Broker auto-import is broad and reliable.** You can pretty much set it and forget it for most US retail brokers and futures platforms.

**Clean mobile app.** You can review the day's trades on your phone without wanting to throw it.

## Honest Cons

**No free tier.** Zero. If you want to try TraderSync, you're on a trial or you're paying. Tradervue and a couple of competitors offer free tiers, so this is a real friction point for beginners just deciding whether to journal at all.

**Elite is expensive.** $79.95/month is a lot for what is fundamentally a trading journal, even one with unlimited AI. Unless you specifically need ultra-precise replay or you'll actually hammer Cypher AI daily, Premium delivers 90% of the value at 62% of the cost.

**Cypher AI message caps sting on Pro.** Five messages a day means you'll ration them, which defeats the purpose of a conversational assistant. Either commit to Premium (15/day) or go elsewhere.

**No backtesting.** TraderSync is a journal, not a strategy tester. If you want to model a system before trading it, look at Edgewonk (which has some backtest-adjacent tooling) or a dedicated tool like TradingView or QuantConnect.

**No news feed, no paper trading, no education.** TraderSync stays in its lane, which is fine — but don't expect it to replace your broker's platform or an education service.

## Who Should Use TraderSync (and Who Shouldn't)

**Use TraderSync if:** you're an active day trader, options trader, or prop firm trader who already knows journaling matters and wants AI-assisted review plus market replay. Premium at $49.95/month is the sweet spot for most serious traders.

**Don't use TraderSync if:** you're a swing trader making 5 trades a month (overkill — Tradervue's free tier is enough), a total beginner who hasn't decided if you'll stick with trading (no free tier to explore), or someone who wants backtesting bundled with journaling (look at Edgewonk instead).

## How It Compares to Competitors

**TraderSync vs. TradeZella:** TradeZella is the closer competitor — similar pricing, similar AI angle, similar multi-asset support. TradeZella's UI is arguably a touch more polished, but TraderSync's market replay is stronger and its Cypher AI feels more mature. If you're a scalper, TraderSync wins. If you want the shinier interface, TradeZella.

**TraderSync vs. Edgewonk:** Edgewonk is the veteran — no AI, no replay, but deeper custom analytics and a one-time-ish pricing model that ends up cheaper long-term. Edgewonk suits the trader who wants to build their own analytical framework. TraderSync suits the trader who wants the framework already built with AI on top.

## The Bottom Line

TraderSync is a legitimately strong trading journal that earns its place in the top tier of the category. The AI is real, the replay is genuinely differentiated, and the multi-account support at Premium is best-in-class for prop firm traders. The **no-free-tier decision is the biggest weakness** — it puts up a wall for beginners — and the Elite plan is priced for traders who really will use every feature daily.

For most serious active traders, TraderSync Premium at $49.95/month (or $359.64/year) is the plan to pick. It's not the cheapest option, but for the combination of Cypher AI, 1-second replay, and 20-account support, there's nothing else in the category that bundles those three things at that price. If you're a journaling skeptic, start elsewhere. If you're already convinced journaling matters and you want the analytical horsepower, TraderSync is a serious tool worth the money.

Clean mobile app makes on-the-go trade review viable
cons: No free tier — every user is either on trial or paying
Elite plan at $79.95/month is expensive relative to peers
Cypher AI message caps (5/day on Pro, 15/day on Premium) feel restrictive
No backtesting, no paper trading, no news feed
No API access for traders who want to build custom workflows
